message:
feat: add pre-built ACFS base Docker image for accurate installer testing

The checker now uses a purpose-built Docker image (afsc-base:latest) that
mirrors a real ACFS VPS mid-install state:

- Non-root user (afsc-user) with passwordless sudo
- Rust toolchain (cargo, rustc) via rustup
- Node.js via nvm
- All ACFS prerequisite apt packages (git, unzip, jq, python3, etc.)
- Proper PATH including ~/.cargo/bin, ~/.local/bin, node

This eliminates Docker-specific false failures:
- srps "don't run as root" → now runs as afsc-user ✓
- Missing unzip/git/python3/rsync → pre-installed ✓
- 20-30s per-container apt-get overhead → eliminated ✓

The image auto-builds on first run (~2 min) and is cached for subsequent
runs. Falls back to ubuntu:22.04 with per-container apt-get install when
a custom image is configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -344,9 +344,10 @@ impl ContainerManager {
         let exec_opts = CreateExecOptions {
             attach_stdout: Some(true),
             attach_stderr: Some(true),
-            // Allocate a pseudo-TTY so installers that check for a TTY
-            // (rustup, ubs/ast-grep, ohmyzsh) work correctly.
-            tty: Some(true),
+            // NOTE: We do NOT set tty on exec. The container itself has tty:true
+            // which makes /dev/tty available to installers that check for it.
+            // But tty on exec merges stdout/stderr into one stream, which breaks
+            // our CHECKSUM_MISMATCH stderr detection and error classification.
             cmd: Some(command.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()),
             ..Default::default()
         };
